frameworks.name: abstract categorical encoding
frameworks.one_line: category identity encoded as internal cognitive state, dissociated from stimulus and action

frameworks.name: perceptual decision making
frameworks.one_line: categorization framed as a tractable window into decision processes more broadly

frameworks.name: hierarchical cortical processing
frameworks.one_line: transformation from feature encoding to flexible category representations across cortical hierarchy

core_question: How are flexible, abstract category representations encoded and transformed across the primate cortical hierarchy?
key_assumptions: categorical decisions can dissociate category identity from motor action
categories used are arbitrary, learned groupings rather than innate features
monkey single-unit physiology generalizes to primate decision circuits
manipulations: review of training monkeys to group visual stimuli into arbitrary categories
comparison of neural encoding across early visual, parietal, and prefrontal areas
key_findings: early visual areas encode stimulus features while parietal and prefrontal areas show flexible categorical representations
category signals are not rigidly tied to specific stimuli or to the action used to report the choice
interpretations: category representations reflect abstract internal cognitive states downstream of feature encoding
categorization provides a model paradigm for studying abstract decision variables more generally
